page: Hetzner Cloud cpx42 is an x86_64 virtual server powered by an AMD EPYC Genoa processor running at 2.0 GHz. It features 8 vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with a shared CPU allocation model, running under the QEMU hypervisor. The system is equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM, providing approximately 1.95 GB of memory per core. Local storage consists of a bundled 320 GB SSD, and there are no complimentary public IPv4 addresses included.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores placing in the top 25%. Multi-core performance for both stress-ng and Geekbench falls in the average tier. Memory performance is notable, with large-block write bandwidth reaching the top 10% tier and large-block read bandwidth in the top 25% tier, though small-block read bandwidth remains average. For specialized workloads, LLM text generation using 135M and 7B models achieves strong performance in the top 25%, while prompt processing speeds and general database operations remain in the average range.

The cpx42 server offers a high-density compute profile with a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, making it a cost-efficient option for workloads requiring modern CPU instructions like AVX-512 and cryptography acceleration. However, the shared CPU allocation model presents a performance tradeoff, as resources are shared with other tenants, which may introduce variability under sustained multi-core loads.

This server configuration is suitable for workloads such as software compilation, lightweight web serving, text processing, and small-scale LLM text generation. The combination of strong single-core execution and high memory write bandwidth supports tasks like basic database operations and intermediate data processing where dedicated CPU cores are not strictly required.
description: Hetzner Cloud cpx42 is an x86_64 virtual server featuring 8 vCPUs on an AMD EPYC Genoa processor with a shared CPU allocation model. It is configured with 16.0 GB of RAM and a bundled 320 GB SSD for local storage. Operating under the QEMU hypervisor, the server demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance and top-tier large-block memory write bandwidth in benchmarks. However, multi-core performance and database operations remain in the average tier. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and shared CPU model offer a cost-efficient entry point for compute-focused tasks, though resource sharing represents a potential tradeoff for sustained workloads. This server is suited for software compilation, static web serving, and small-scale language model text generation.
og_description: Hetzner Cloud cpx42 is an x86_64 server with 8 shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and a 320 GB SSD, delivering strong single-core performance and high memory write bandwidth.

tagline: An AMD Genoa virtual server featuring eight shared vCPUs, fast memory write speeds, and bundled SSD storage for compute-focused workloads.

Strong single-core CPU and top-tier large-block memory write performance
Shared CPU allocation model under QEMU hypervisor
Suited for software compilation, web serving, and small-scale LLM text generation
